Good morning, Bruno. Let me present the numbers for this month.
Great. How is the total of sales?
The total is bigger than last month. And our profit is higher too.
Very good. And the growth this year?
The growth is good. Sugar exports increase every year.
The numbers are great, Anna. Thank you for the report.

Good morning, team. Let me present the numbers for this month. The total of sales is bigger than last month. Our profit is higher too. The growth is good. Sugar exports are bigger than last year. In my opinion, the numbers are very good. Next month, we want a bigger total. Thank you, and let us look at the chart together.

Today I presented the numbers to the team. I was a little nervous. But I used easy English. I said, the total is bigger than last month. The profit is higher. The growth is good. My colleagues understood me. Bruno said, the numbers are great, Anna. Now I feel confident with numbers in English. I can present a report to my team.

First, Anna opens the report and shows the total of sales. Then she says the total is bigger than last month. Next, she shows the profit is higher this month. After that, she talks about the growth this year. Finally, she ends the report and the team agrees the numbers are good.
